**Workbook filters:** **Time Range** and **User ID** apply across the workbook. resources.name: [concat(parameters('workspace'),'/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/',concat('Workbook-', last(split(variables('workbookId1'),'/'))))]
resources.description: The StealthTalk Anomalous Auth Monitor workbook provides a real-time SOC dashboard with five sections: Overview (5 KPI tiles + timeline + composite User Risk Leaderboard + Multi-Vector Correlation), Off-Hours, New Devices, Geo Anomaly, and Brute Force. Includes an interactive World Map and per-user forensic tables.

properties.description: AfterHoursWork_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.0.0

Identifies systematic off-hours activity for a single StealthTalk user - repeated authentications
outside the user's configured working hours OR on weekends, observed across at least two
distinct calendar days within a 48-hour window. The pattern is a common indicator of credential
misuse, insider threat, or compromise of the account by an attacker operating in a different
timezone.

An "off-hours event" is one where IsWeekend=true OR DeviationMinutes >= 180 (i.e. >= 3 hours
after the configured working-hours end). Three or more such events on at least two distinct
days are required for an incident to fire - a single late-evening login is not enough.
properties.query:
let LookbackPeriod    = 48h;
let MinAttempts       = 3;
let MinDistinctDays   = 2;
let OffHoursThreshold = 180;
StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(LookbackPeriod)
| where EventType == "OffHoursLogin"
| where IsWeekend == true or DeviationMinutes >= OffHoursThreshold
| summarize
    AttemptCount   = count(),
    DistinctDays   = dcount(startofday(TimeGenerated)),
    FirstSeen      = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen       = max(TimeGenerated),
    DeviceIds      = make_set(DeviceId),
    MaxDeviation   = max(DeviationMinutes),
    WeekendsCount  = countif(IsWeekend == true),
    AppVersions    = make_set(AppVersion)
  by UserId
| where AttemptCount >= MinAttempts and DistinctDays >= MinDistinctDays
| extend
    AlertName    = "AfterHoursWork",
    AlertDetails = strcat(
        "User ", UserId,
        " performed ", AttemptCount, " off-hours logins",
        " across ", DistinctDays, " distinct days.",
        " Max deviation from working hours: ", MaxDeviation, " min.",
        " Weekend logins: ", WeekendsCount, "."
    )
| project
    TimeGenerated = LastSeen,
    UserId, AttemptCount, DistinctDays, MaxDeviation,
    WeekendsCount, DeviceIds, AppVersions, FirstSeen, LastSeen,
    AlertName, AlertDetails

properties.description:
Identifies a single StealthTalk user registering two or more previously-unseen devices within
a 24-hour window. Sudden registration of multiple new devices is a strong indicator of
account takeover (an attacker enrolling their own device after credential theft) or
device-farm abuse.

The rule fires when a user produces two or more distinct NewDeviceId values within the
lookback window, regardless of operating system. Look at the OSList custom detail to spot
cross-platform patterns (e.g. an iOS-only user suddenly enrolling an Android device).
properties.query:
let LookbackPeriod = 24h;
let MinNewDevices  = 2;
StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(LookbackPeriod)
| where EventType == "NewDeviceLogin"
| summarize
    NewDeviceCount  = dcount(NewDeviceId),
    NewDevices      = make_set(NewDeviceId),
    NewDeviceOSList = make_set(NewDeviceOS),
    FirstSeen       = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen        = max(TimeGenerated),
    AppVersions     = make_set(AppVersion)
  by UserId
| where NewDeviceCount >= MinNewDevices
| extend
    AlertName    = "MultiNewDevicesRegistration",
    AlertDetails = strcat(
        "User ", UserId,
        " registered ", NewDeviceCount, " new devices within 24 hours.",
        " Device IDs: ", tostring(NewDevices), ".",
        " Operating systems: ", tostring(NewDeviceOSList), "."
    )
| project
    TimeGenerated = LastSeen,
    UserId, NewDeviceCount, NewDevices, NewDeviceOSList,
    FirstSeen, LastSeen, AppVersions, AlertName, AlertDetails

alertDetailsOverride.alertDisplayNameFormat: StealthTalk: Multi New Devices - {{UserId}} ({{NewDeviceCount}} new devices)

properties.description: LoginOutsideWorkZone_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.0.0

Identifies a single StealthTalk authentication originating from a country or city that does
not match the user's assigned (expected) geographic zone. Each individual mismatch is
treated as an incident - there is no aggregation threshold, since a single login from an
unexpected country is high-confidence evidence of a credential issue.

An incident fires when LoginCountry differs from AssignedCountry OR LoginCity differs
from AssignedCity. Source IPv4, raw event ID, and both the observed and expected
geo-locations are surfaced as entities and custom details for the SOC analyst.
properties.query:
let LookbackPeriod = 1h;
StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(LookbackPeriod)
| where EventType == "GeoAnomalyLogin"
| where LoginCountry != AssignedCountry
     or LoginCity    != AssignedCity
| extend
    CountryMismatch = LoginCountry != AssignedCountry,
    CityMismatch    = LoginCity    != AssignedCity,
    AlertName       = "LoginOutsideWorkZone",
    AlertDetails    = strcat(
        "User ", UserId,
        " logged in from ", LoginCity, " (", LoginCountry, ")",
        " - assigned zone: ", AssignedCity, " (", AssignedCountry, ").",
        " Source IP: ", IpAddress, ".",
        " Country mismatch: ", tostring(LoginCountry != AssignedCountry), ".",
        " City mismatch: ", tostring(LoginCity != AssignedCity), "."
    )
| project
    TimeGenerated, UserId, DeviceId,
    LoginCountry, LoginCity, AssignedCountry, AssignedCity,
    CountryMismatch, CityMismatch, IpAddress,
    AppVersion, RawEventId, AlertName, AlertDetails

alertDetailsOverride.alertDisplayNameFormat: StealthTalk: Login Outside Work Zone - {{UserId}} from {{LoginCity}} ({{LoginCountry}})

properties.description: PasswordBruteForce_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.0.0

Identifies a brute-force authentication attempt against a single StealthTalk user account. The StealthTalk anti-brute-force subsystem emits a `MultiFailLogin` event when consecutive failed attempts trigger an automatic account lockout. This rule fires when the reported number of consecutive failures is 9 or more, distinguishing a credential-guessing attack from an isolated user error. The lockout duration (`LoginBlockingSeconds`) is surfaced as a custom detail so the SOC analyst can prioritise by attack persistence.
properties.query:
let LookbackPeriod    = 5h;
let MinFailedAttempts = 9;
StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(LookbackPeriod)
| where EventType == "MultiFailLogin"
| where PassedAttempts >= MinFailedAttempts
| extend
    BlockingMinutes = LoginBlockingSeconds / 60,
    AlertName       = "PasswordBruteForce",
    AlertDetails    = strcat(
        "User ", UserId,
        " had ", PassedAttempts, " consecutive failed login attempts",
        " on device ", DeviceId, ".",
        " Account blocked for ", LoginBlockingSeconds, " seconds",
        " (", LoginBlockingSeconds / 60, " min)."
    )
| project
    TimeGenerated, UserId, DeviceId,
    PassedAttempts, LoginBlockingSeconds, BlockingMinutes,
    AppVersion, RawEventId, AlertName, AlertDetails

alertDetailsOverride.alertDisplayNameFormat: StealthTalk: Password Brute Force - {{UserId}} ({{PassedAttempts}} failed attempts)

properties.description: ImpossibleTravel_HuntingQueries Hunting Query with template version 3.0.0

properties.query:
let lookback = 7d;
StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(lookback)
| where EventType == "GeoAnomalyLogin"
| project TimeGenerated, UserId, LoginCountry, LoginCity, IpAddress
| order by UserId asc, TimeGenerated asc
| serialize
| extend
    PrevUser    = prev(UserId),
    PrevTime    = prev(TimeGenerated),
    PrevCountry = prev(LoginCountry),
    PrevIP      = prev(IpAddress)
| where UserId == PrevUser
| where LoginCountry != PrevCountry
| where TimeGenerated - PrevTime <= 30m
| project
    UserId,
    FirstCountry   = PrevCountry,
    SecondCountry  = LoginCountry,
    FirstTime      = PrevTime,
    SecondTime     = TimeGenerated,
    MinutesBetween = datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, PrevTime),
    FirstIP        = PrevIP,
    SecondIP       = IpAddress
| extend AlertName = "ImpossibleTravel"
| order by MinutesBetween asc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Identifies a single StealthTalk user authenticating from two different countries within a 30-minute window. Legitimate physical travel between countries cannot occur on that timescale, so this is a strong indicator of stolen credentials.

properties.description: AccountTakeoverSequence_HuntingQueries Hunting Query with template version 3.0.0

properties.query:
let lookback   = 7d;
let TimeWindow = 24h;
let offHours = StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL
    | where TimeGenerated >= ago(lookback)
    | where EventType == "OffHoursLogin"
    | project UserId, T_offhours = TimeGenerated;
let newDevice = StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL
    | where TimeGenerated >= ago(lookback)
    | where EventType == "NewDeviceLogin"
    | project UserId, T_device = TimeGenerated, NewDeviceId;
let geoAnomaly = StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL
    | where TimeGenerated >= ago(lookback)
    | where EventType == "GeoAnomalyLogin"
    | project UserId, T_geo = TimeGenerated, LoginCountry, IpAddress;
offHours
| join kind=inner newDevice  on UserId
| join kind=inner geoAnomaly on UserId
| where T_device between ((T_offhours - TimeWindow) .. (T_offhours + TimeWindow))
| where T_geo    between ((T_offhours - TimeWindow) .. (T_offhours + TimeWindow))
| project UserId, T_offhours, T_device, T_geo, NewDeviceId, LoginCountry, IpAddress
| extend
    AlertName = "AccountTakeoverSequence",
    RiskLevel = "Critical",
    StartTime = min_of(T_offhours, T_device, T_geo),
    EndTime   = max_of(T_offhours, T_device, T_geo)
| distinct UserId, AlertName, RiskLevel, LoginCountry, IpAddress, NewDeviceId, T_offhours, T_device, T_geo, StartTime, EndTime

tags.name: description
tags.value: Identifies the classic three-stage account-takeover pattern for the same StealthTalk user within 24 hours: off-hours login, new device registration, and geo-anomaly login. All three together is high-confidence evidence of credential compromise.

properties.displayName: StealthTalk - Brute force followed by suspicious access
properties.query:
let lookback = 7d;
let bfWindow = 2h;
let PostEventsByUser = materialize(
    StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL
    | where TimeGenerated >= ago(lookback)
    | where EventType in ("GeoAnomalyLogin", "NewDeviceLogin")
    | project UserId, PostEvent_Time = TimeGenerated, PostEventType = EventType
    | summarize PostEvents = make_list(pack("PostEvent_Time", PostEvent_Time, "PostEventType", PostEventType)) by UserId
);
StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(lookback)
| where EventType == "MultiFailLogin"
| where PassedAttempts >= 9
| project UserId, BF_Time = TimeGenerated, DeviceId, PassedAttempts
| join kind=inner PostEventsByUser on UserId
| mv-apply PostEvent = PostEvents on (
    extend
        PostEvent_Time = todatetime(PostEvent.PostEvent_Time),
        PostEventType  = tostring(PostEvent.PostEventType)
    | where PostEvent_Time > BF_Time
    | where (PostEvent_Time - BF_Time) <= bfWindow
    | top 1 by PostEvent_Time asc
  )
| project
    UserId,
    BruteForceAt    = BF_Time,
    Attempts        = PassedAttempts,
    SuspectEventAt  = PostEvent_Time,
    SuspectType     = PostEventType
| extend AlertName = "BruteForceFollowedBySuspiciousAccess"
| order by BruteForceAt desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Identifies credential-stuffing or brute-force success: a MultiFailLogin event with 9+ failed attempts followed within 2 hours by a GeoAnomalyLogin or NewDeviceLogin for the same user - the closest proxy for "the brute-force eventually succeeded".

properties.displayName: StealthTalk - Brute force followed by suspicious access

connectorUiConfig.title: StealthTalk Anomalous Authentication
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown:
The StealthTalk Anomalous Authentication connector ingests four classes of anomalous user-authentication events from the StealthTalk Enterprise messenger into a custom Log Analytics table via the Logs Ingestion API.

Events:
- **OffHoursLogin** - login outside the user's configured working hours.
- **NewDeviceLogin** - login from a previously-unseen device.
- **GeoAnomalyLogin** - login from a country/city that does not match the user's assigned geo zone.
- **MultiFailLogin** - multiple consecutive failed authentication attempts.

sampleQueries.description: All anomalous-auth events in the last 24 hours
sampleQueries.query:
StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| order by TimeGenerated desc

sampleQueries.description: Top users by event count (last 7 days)
sampleQueries.query:
StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| summarize EventCount = count() by UserId
| top 20 by EventCount desc

sampleQueries.description: StealthTalk events visible through the standard ASIM Authentication union
sampleQueries.query:
imAuthentication()
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where EventVendor == "StealthTalk"

dataTypes.name: StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL
| summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
| where isnotempty(Time)

value:
StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL
| summarize LastEvent = max(TimeGenerated)
| project IsConnected = iff(isnull(LastEvent), false, LastEvent > ago(7d))

customs.name: StealthTalk Enterprise instance
customs.description: A deployed StealthTalk Enterprise tenant with admin access to configure the outgoing webhook to the Log Analytics workspace.

customs.name: Microsoft Entra App Registration
customs.description: A service principal that StealthTalk uses to authenticate to the Logs Ingestion API. Must have the **Monitoring Metrics Publisher** role on the deployed Data Collection Rule.

instructionSteps.description:
When the Solution is installed via the Microsoft Sentinel Content Hub, the following resources are deployed automatically into the workspace's resource group:

1. **Custom log table** `StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL` (21-field stream).
2. **Data Collection Endpoint** `StealthTalk-Sentinel-DCE`.
3. **Data Collection Rule** `StealthTalk-Sentinel-DCR` with `Custom-StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL` stream declaration and a one-to-one transform.
4. **ASIM Authentication 0.1.3 parsers** as workspace saved-search functions: `vimAuthenticationStealthTalk` (filtering) and `ASimAuthenticationStealthTalk` (non-filtering).
5. **`imAuthentication` extension parser** that registers the StealthTalk source in the workspace ASIM Authentication union (overrides Microsoft's `imAuthentication` saved-search to include the StealthTalk vim parser).
6. **`ASimDisabledParsers` watchlist** + Microsoft Sentinel onboarding state (idempotent).

No user action is required at this step beyond installing the Solution. The remaining steps configure StealthTalk to push events into the deployed DCR. Pre-requisite: Microsoft's `FullDeploymentAuthentication.json` ASIM Authentication parser pack must be deployed in the workspace before this Solution is installed.
instructionSteps.title: STEP 1 - Auto-deployed ingestion infrastructure (review only)

instructionSteps.description:
In the Azure portal, open the Data Collection Endpoint and Data Collection Rule resources deployed in Step 1:

- From the **Data Collection Endpoint** properties, copy the **Logs Ingestion endpoint URI** (looks like `https://stealthtalk-sentinel-dce-<suffix>.<region>.ingest.monitor.azure.com`).
- From the **Data Collection Rule** Overview, copy the **Immutable ID** (looks like `dcr-<guid>`).

You will paste both values into the StealthTalk admin console in Step 5.
instructionSteps.title: STEP 2 - Capture the DCE endpoint and DCR Immutable ID

instructionSteps.description: Create a new App Registration in your Microsoft Entra tenant. Generate a client secret (or use a certificate). Note the **Tenant ID**, **App (client) ID** and **Client secret**. StealthTalk uses this service principal to authenticate to the Logs Ingestion API via OAuth.
instructionSteps.title: STEP 3 - Create a Microsoft Entra App Registration for StealthTalk

instructionSteps.description:
Open the Data Collection Rule deployed in Step 1 -> **Access control (IAM)** -> **+ Add role assignment**.

- Role: **Monitoring Metrics Publisher**
- Assign access to: **User, group, or service principal**
- Select the App Registration from Step 3

RBAC role assignments cannot be performed by the Solution install template, so this step is manual.
instructionSteps.title: STEP 4 - Grant the App Registration the Monitoring Metrics Publisher role on the DCR

instructionSteps.description:
In the StealthTalk admin console, configure the Microsoft Sentinel/Log Analytics integration with the values captured in Steps 2 and 3:

- **DCE endpoint** - the Logs Ingestion endpoint URI from Step 2
- **DCR Immutable ID** - from Step 2
- **Stream name** - `Custom-StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL`
- **Tenant ID, App (client) ID, Client secret** - from Step 3

Save and trigger a test event. The StealthTalk console reports success/failure of the OAuth handshake and the first event delivery.
instructionSteps.title: STEP 5 - Configure StealthTalk to send events

instructionSteps.description:
Run the **Sample queries** above in **Logs**. All must return rows within a few minutes of test events:

- Raw `StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL` table populated.
- ASIM union view via `imAuthentication() | where EventVendor == 'StealthTalk'` returns the same events normalised to the ASIM Authentication schema.

If the raw table is empty: verify the StealthTalk console reports successful OAuth and event delivery, and confirm the App Registration has the **Monitoring Metrics Publisher** role on the DCR (Step 4). If the raw table has rows but ASIM union is empty: re-check that Microsoft's `FullDeploymentAuthentication.json` parser pack is deployed.
instructionSteps.title: STEP 6 - Verify ingestion and ASIM normalisation

instructionSteps.description: In the Microsoft Sentinel **Content hub**, open the **StealthTalk Anomalous Authentication** Solution and click **Manage**. Enable the four Analytic Rule templates, the three Hunting Query templates, the Workbook, and the Microsoft Teams Playbook. For the Playbook, after deployment, grant its system-assigned managed identity the **Microsoft Sentinel Responder** role on the workspace, and grant the **Azure Security Insights** service principal the **Microsoft Sentinel Automation Contributor** role on the playbook's resource group. Then create an **Automation Rule** in Microsoft Sentinel that runs the playbook on incidents from the four StealthTalk analytic rules.
instructionSteps.title: STEP 7 - Enable analytic rules, hunting queries, workbook, and playbook

properties.descriptionHtml:
Note: Please refer to the following before installing the solution:

• Review the solution Release Notes

• There may be known issues pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing.

The StealthTalk Anomalous Authentication solution ingests StealthTalk Enterprise sign-in anomaly data into Microsoft Sentinel so SOC teams can investigate anomalous authentication activity alongside Microsoft security telemetry. The solution includes a Logs Ingestion API data connector, scheduled analytic rules, hunting queries, ASIM authentication parsers, a workbook, and a Microsoft Teams playbook. It depends on Microsoft Sentinel, Azure Monitor Logs Ingestion, custom tables, Logic Apps, and ASIM.

Data Connectors: 1, Workbooks: 1, Analytic Rules: 4, Hunting Queries: 3, Playbooks: 1

Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel | Learn more about Solutions

metadata._comment_purpose: Creates the empty 'ASimDisabledParsers' watchlist required by Microsoft's full ASIM Authentication deployment. The standard MS imAuthentication() union calls _GetWatchlist('ASimDisabledParsers') in its body - and the _GetWatchlist function is registered in a workspace only after at least one watchlist exists. Without this watchlist, executing imAuthentication() in a clean workspace fails with: Unknown function: '_GetWatchlist'.
metadata._comment_when_to_run: Deploy this AFTER Microsoft's FullDeploymentAuthentication.json and BEFORE the StealthTalk imAuthentication_extension.json. After it is deployed, run 'imAuthentication() | take 1' in Logs to verify the chain works.
metadata._comment_content: The watchlist is created with two columns ('SKey' and 'Description') and one placeholder data row 'None,Placeholder row required by watchlist API'. KEY-NAMING TRICK: in apiVersion 2023-02-01+ the column literally named 'SearchKey' is rejected with 'ItemsKeyValue should not contain SearchKey as a key' - the name conflicts with the API's internal itemsSearchKey metadata field. We use 'SKey' in the CSV and set itemsSearchKey to 'SKey' instead. In watchlist queries the key is still surfaced as 'SearchKey' (the watchlist function maps itemsSearchKey -> SearchKey), so the standard MS imAuthentication() filter shape ('| where SearchKey in (...)') remains compatible. The disabled-parsers machinery therefore no-ops by default not because 'SearchKey' is missing, but because the only seeded key value is the inert placeholder 'None', which does not match any real ASIM parser exclusion key. The Sentinel watchlist API also rejects rawContent on two other cases that our format avoids: header-only files (Invalid csv content: no watchlist items found), and rows where the only column is the itemsSearchKey (after extracting the key, the item dict is empty).
metadata._comment_sentinel_onboarding: The template also onboards Microsoft Sentinel to the Log Analytics workspace if not already onboarded. ARM upsert on Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces/providers/onboardingStates with name 'default' is idempotent - running it on an already-onboarded workspace is a no-op. Without onboarding any Microsoft.SecurityInsights/* resource (watchlists, alert rules, automation rules) fails with 'Workspace is not onboarded to Microsoft Sentinel'.

metadata.description: Name of the existing Log Analytics / Microsoft Sentinel workspace where the watchlist should be created.

properties.description: ASIM watchlist used to selectively disable individual source parsers (e.g. ExcludevimAuthenticationXxx). Empty by default - its mere existence registers the _GetWatchlist function in the workspace, which is required by MS-built ASIM union functions like imAuthentication().
properties.rawContent:
SKey,Description
None,Placeholder row required by watchlist API

metadata._comment_purpose: Deploys the StealthTalk anomalous-auth ingestion pipeline into a Log Analytics / Microsoft Sentinel workspace. Installs: 1) custom log table StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL (name is locked; rest of the solution content references it by literal name so it must not diverge), 2) Data Collection Endpoint, 3) Data Collection Rule with stream declaration and a one-to-one transform.
metadata._comment_post_deploy: After deploy, open the DCR -> Access control (IAM) -> add role 'Monitoring Metrics Publisher' to the App Registration that StealthTalk uses to authenticate. Role-assignment is intentionally NOT included in this template because Solution-installed templates cannot grant RBAC.

Name of the Data Collection Endpoint to be created.
Name of the Data Collection Rule to be created.
Azure region. Defaults to the resource group's region.
Retention for the custom log table in days. Must be >= the workspace minimum (4 by default).

properties.query:
let Parser = (
 starttime : datetime = datetime(null),
 endtime : datetime = datetime(null),
 username_has_any : dynamic = dynamic([]),
 targetappname_has_any : dynamic = dynamic([]),
 srcipaddr_has_any_prefix : dynamic = dynamic([]),
 srchostname_has_any : dynamic = dynamic([]),
 eventtype_in : dynamic = dynamic([]),
 eventresultdetails_in : dynamic = dynamic([]),
 eventresult : string = '*',
 disabled : bool = false
) {
 StealthTalkAnomalousAuth_CL
 | where not(disabled)
 | where (isnull(starttime) or TimeGenerated >= starttime)
 and (isnull(endtime) or TimeGenerated <= endtime)
 | extend
 _OriginalEventType = EventType,
 _EventResult = iff(EventType == 'MultiFailLogin', 'Failure', 'Success')
 | where (eventresult == '*' or _EventResult =~ eventresult)
 | where (array_length(username_has_any) == 0
 or UserId has_any (username_has_any))
 | where (array_length(srcipaddr_has_any_prefix) == 0
 or has_any_ipv4_prefix(IpAddress, srcipaddr_has_any_prefix))
 | where (array_length(eventtype_in) == 0
 or 'Logon' in~ (eventtype_in))
 | extend
 EventSchemaVersion = '0.1.3',
 EventSchema = 'Authentication',
 EventProduct = 'StealthTalk',
 EventVendor = 'StealthTalk',
 EventType = 'Logon',
 EventResult = _EventResult,
 EventCount = int(1),
 EventStartTime = TimeGenerated,
 EventEndTime = TimeGenerated,
 EventUid = RawEventId,
 EventSeverity = case(
 Severity == 'High', 'High',
 Severity == 'Medium', 'Medium',
 Severity == 'Low', 'Low',
 'Informational'
 ),
 EventResultDetails = case(
 _OriginalEventType == 'MultiFailLogin',
 strcat('Failed attempts: ', tostring(PassedAttempts), ', blocked for: ', tostring(LoginBlockingSeconds), 's'),
 _OriginalEventType == 'GeoAnomalyLogin',
 strcat('Login from ', LoginCity, ' (', LoginCountry, '), assigned: ', AssignedCity, ' (', AssignedCountry, ')'),
 _OriginalEventType == 'NewDeviceLogin',
 strcat('New device: ', NewDeviceId, ', OS: ', NewDeviceOS),
 _OriginalEventType == 'OffHoursLogin',
 strcat('Deviation: ', tostring(DeviationMinutes), ' min, weekend: ', tostring(IsWeekend)),
 ''
 ),
 EventOriginalType = _OriginalEventType,
 TargetUserId = UserId,
 TargetUserIdType = 'Other',
 TargetUsername = UserId,
 TargetUsernameType = 'Other',
 SrcDeviceId = DeviceId,

metadata._comment_purpose: Overrides the workspace function 'imAuthentication' (deployed by MS FullDeploymentAuthentication.json) so that the StealthTalk vim parser participates in the ASIM Authentication union. Re-deploys the function with the same FunctionAlias and uses etag='*' to overwrite the existing saved-search.
metadata._comment_signature: Public signature matches the Microsoft contract: (starttime, endtime, username_has_any, targetappname_has_any, srcipaddr_has_any_prefix, srchostname_has_any, eventtype_in, eventresultdetails_in, eventresult, pack). 'pack' (not 'disabled') is the public param; 'disabled' is computed per-source from the ASimDisabledParsers watchlist.
metadata._comment_source_list: Source list synchronized with the actual MS body deployed in stealthtalk-analytics-security workspace (May 2026). 36 built-in MS source parsers + 1 custom (vimAuthenticationStealthTalk).
metadata._comment_maintenance: When Microsoft updates FullDeploymentAuthentication.json (adds/removes source parsers), open Functions -> imAuthentication -> Edit, copy the new union list, and re-sync the body below. Keep vimAuthenticationStealthTalk(...) as the LAST entry in the union, classified under imAuthenticationCustomDisabled.
metadata._comment_override_rationale: Using etag:'*' to overwrite the MS-deployed imAuthentication function is the documented ASIM extension pattern for partner-published parsers. The alternative — exposing a separate StealthTalk-aware union function — was considered and rejected because downstream consumers (built-in MS analytic rules, hunting queries, Workbook ASIM panels, third-party content) all call imAuthentication() by name; introducing a parallel function would make StealthTalk events invisible to that ecosystem and defeat the purpose of being an ASIM-compliant source.
metadata._comment_update_process: Update procedure (run on each MS ASIM release): (1) In the workspace, run `.show function imAuthentication | project Parameters, Body` to capture the latest MS body; (2) Diff against the body in this file; (3) If MS added/removed source parsers in their union, sync the changes here, keeping vimAuthenticationStealthTalk last; (4) Bump contentVersion (line 3) by one patch digit; (5) Re-deploy via this template — etag:'*' will overwrite the workspace function safely; (6) Verify with `imAuthentication() | where EventVendor == 'StealthTalk' | take 1`. This update is required only on MS ASIM Authentication minor/major releases (~quarterly cadence per MS ASIM SIG); built-in source parser version bumps inside the same MS release do not require a re-sync.
metadata._comment_monitoring: Sentinel Health workbook (built-in) reports when ASIM parser functions are modified. The override is logged once at deploy time; subsequent MS FullDeploymentAuthentication.json re-deploys may revert this extension (last-writer-wins on etag:'*'), in which case re-run this template to restore the StealthTalk entry.

metadata.description: Name of the Log Analytics / Microsoft Sentinel workspace (e.g. stealthtalk-analytics-security).
